input.prompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

input.initialPrompt:
I need a CollectionView that supports single item selection and
pull-to-refresh. When the user selects an item, I want to navigate
to a detail page. When they pull to refresh, I want to reload data
from my API. Show me the XAML and explain the ViewModel bindings needed.

data.content:
Here's a complete example:

**XAML**
```xml
<CollectionView
    ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
    SelectionMode="Single"
    SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem}"
    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}"
    RemainingItemsThreshold="0">

    <CollectionView.Behaviors>
        <toolkit:EventToCommandBehavior
            EventName="SelectionChanged"
            Command="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}" />
    </CollectionView.Behaviors>

    <!-- Pull-to-refresh: wrap in RefreshView -->
</CollectionView>
```

In practice, wrap the `CollectionView` in a `RefreshView` for pull-to-refresh:

```xml
<RefreshView
    Command="{Binding RefreshCommand}"
    IsRefreshing="{Binding IsRefreshing}">

    <CollectionView
        ItemsSource="{Binding Items}"
        SelectionMode="Single"
        SelectedItem="{Binding SelectedItem, Mode=TwoWay}"
        SelectionChangedCommand="{Binding SelectionChangedCommand}">

        <CollectionView.ItemTemplate>
            <DataTemplate x:DataType="models:MyItem">
                <Grid Padding="10">
                    <Label Text="{Binding Name}" FontSize="16" />
                </Grid>
            </DataTemplate>
        </CollectionView.ItemTemplate>

    </CollectionView>
</RefreshView>
```

**ViewModel**
```csharp
public partial class MyViewModel : ObservableObject
{
    private readonly IApiService _api;
    private readonly INavigationService _nav;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private ObservableCollection<MyItem> _items = new();

    [ObservableProperty]
    private MyItem? _selectedItem;

    [ObservableProperty]
    private bool _isRefreshing;

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task RefreshAsync()
    {
        try
        {
            var data = await _api.GetItemsAsync();
            Items = new ObservableCollection<MyItem>(data);
        }
        finally
        {
            IsRefreshing = false;   // stops the spinner
        }
    }

    [RelayCommand]
    private async Task SelectionChangedAsync()
    {
        if (SelectedItem is null) return;

        await _nav.GoToAsync(nameof(DetailPage),
            new Dictionary<string, object> { ["Item"] = SelectedItem });

        SelectedItem = null;        // clear so re-selection works
    }
}
```

**Key bindings explained:**

| Binding | Direction | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `ItemsSource → Items` | One-way | Drives the list content |
| `SelectedItem ↔ SelectedItem` | **Two-way** | Tracks selection; set to `null` after navigation so the same item can be re-selected |
| `RefreshView.Command → RefreshCommand` | One-way | Fires on pull-to-refresh |
| `RefreshView.IsRefreshing ↔ IsRefreshing` | Two-way | Set `false` when load completes to dismiss the spinner |
| `SelectionChangedCommand` | One-way | Navigates to the detail page |

**Important notes:**
- `SelectedItem` must be **TwoWay** and reset to `null` after navigation, otherwise tapping the same item twice won't fire again.
- `IsRefreshing = false` belongs in a `finally` block so the spinner always stops, even on errors.
- `CollectionView` itself has no `Command` for refresh — you must wrap it in a `RefreshView`.
data.reasoningText: The user is asking about creating a CollectionView in .NET MAUI (or Xamarin.Forms) with single item selection and pull-to-refresh, along with XAML and ViewModel binding explanations. This is a straightforward knowledge question — no codebase exploration needed.
